meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing to overcome project paralysis. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first portfolio app.
content.answer_block: Overcome project paralysis by building a SaaS pricing calculator or tiering engine. This project forces you to learn database schemas, dynamic UI logic, and subscription modeling. By focusing on the business logic of pricing first, you transform a blank screen into a functional, marketable tool for your developer portfolio.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the weight of 'no project ideas' hanging heavy in the air. You want to build, but the path from zero to a deployed SaaS feels obscured by a fog of abstraction. You aren't just lacking an idea; you are lacking a concrete technical challenge that bridges the gap between tutorial hell and real-world engineering.

Take a breath. The secret isn't a revolutionary startup concept—it's mastering the mechanics of a SaaS pricing engine. By treating your first project as a business-logic exercise, you bypass the creative block and dive straight into the architecture that powers every major software company. Let's turn your confusion into a high-value portfolio piece.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish the database structure for 'Free', 'Pro', and 'Enterprise' tiers using a standard relational model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean SQL schema for a SaaS pricing system with tables for plans, features, and user subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a Prisma schema for SaaS plans and user subscriptions'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Create the backend logic to calculate annual vs. monthly billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript function that calculates recurring revenue based on plan selection and billing interval.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor pricing logic into a utility module with unit tests'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Design a responsive pricing toggle component that updates card values dynamically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Tailwind CSS for a pricing toggle that switches between monthly and annual plans.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a responsive PricingTable component with state-managed billing toggles'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Mocking
steps.description: Simulate the checkout flow to understand how external payment APIs interact with your local state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a mock Stripe checkout handler that validates plan IDs before redirecting to a success page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a mock payment flow using a simulated API route'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your pricing engine to a live environment to verify the end-to-end user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for preparing this SaaS pricing project for deployment on Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment configuration file for Vercel'

pro_tips: Always build your pricing tiers as an array of objects in a configuration file, not hard-coded in your UI components.
Use 'Feature Flags' to toggle new pricing tiers on and off without redeploying your entire application.
Never store currency values as floats; always use integers representing cents to avoid floating-point math errors.
Implement a 'Most Popular' badge logic using conditional CSS classes to practice UI state management.
Track conversion rates by logging which pricing tier button the user hovers over before clicking.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, integrate a real Stripe API key to handle actual transactions and add a user authentication layer using NextAuth.js.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build any SaaS. Don't let this knowledge sit idle—ship your project, share it on Twitter, and get feedback from the community. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
